name: Antigravity CLI
summary: Free terminal coding agent that runs multiple sub-agents in the background, so you can fire off big refactors or parallel research without locking up your session. Ships as a single Go binary and replaces the retired Gemini CLI for individual devs.

name: Agent Finder for Copilot
summary: Describe a coding task in plain English and Copilot searches a catalog of agent tools, MCP servers, and skills, then surfaces ranked matches it can pull in on demand — nothing gets wired up without your say-so. Free for all Copilot plans.

name: MiniMax Hub
summary: Make a whole short film in one app — image, video, voiceover, music, and editing together, no juggling separate tools. Built for solo creators, with indie filmmakers already shipping shorts on it.

name: Kimi K2.7-Code
summary: Open-weight coding agent you can self-host or hit cheaply via API — it plans, executes, and debugs across long sessions. Trillion-param Mixture-of-Experts weights are on Hugging Face under a commercial-friendly MIT-style license.

name: DiffusionGemma
summary: Open-weight text model that runs locally on a single GPU (18GB VRAM) and generates up to 4x faster using diffusion. Self-host it free for fast drafting, autocomplete, or local agents — Apache 2.0 weights are on Hugging Face.

name: Claude Fable 5
summary: A new top tier above Opus, with state-of-the-art coding, knowledge work, and vision plus million-token context. Live now on claude.ai, Claude Code, the API, and Bedrock for Pro and Max subscribers.

name: Apple Foundation Models free tier
summary: Build AI features into your iOS app and call on-device and cloud models for free if you're a small dev (under 2M lifetime downloads). Now with image input and a Swift API that can route to Claude or Gemini.
